title: Triple Barrier Meta-Labeling
summary: Meta-labeling, introduced by Marcos López de Prado in 'Advances in Financial Machine Learning', is a two-stage approach that separates the question of trade direction from the question of whether to take a trade at all. A primary model generates directional signals (long/short), and a secondary ML classifier is trained only to predict if each signal will be a winner — effectively filtering out false positives. The training labels are assigned via the Triple Barrier Method: a profit target, a stop-loss, and a maximum holding period define three barriers; whichever is hit first determines the label (1=win, -1=loss, 0=timeout). Empirical results from Hudson & Thames and a 2025 MDPI crypto study show consistent improvement in Sharpe ratio and win rate because the meta-model learns the market conditions under which the primary signal is reliable. This is distinct from Decifer's existing 9-dimension score, which combines signals linearly — meta-labeling adds a trained non-linear confidence gate on top of the aggregated score.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — ML Feature Engineering & Predictive Signal Construction
